message:
chore(beads): update bd-1yt6 status from open to in_progress

The cancellation reliability issue (bd-1yt6) has active development
underway. Update the beads issue tracker to reflect the new status
and bump the updated_at timestamp to 2026-02-19T06:12:06Z, signaling
that implementation work on remote build cancellation with
deterministic cleanup semantics is now in progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1yj8","title":"Test: Exit Code Preservation & Propagation","description":"## Purpose
Test that compilation exit codes are correctly propagated from remote workers to the local caller.

## Test Cases

### Success Exit Codes
1. Build success (exit 0):
   - `cargo build` on valid project
   - Verify: rch returns exit 0

2. Test success (exit 0):
   - `cargo test` all pass
   - Verify: rch returns exit 0

### Failure Exit Codes
3. Build error (exit 1):
   - `cargo build` on broken code
   - Verify: rch returns exit 1

4. Test failures (exit 101):
   - `cargo test` with failing tests
   - Verify: rch returns exit 101 exactly

5. Signal exit (128+N):
   - Kill compilation with SIGTERM
   - Verify: rch returns 143 (128+15)

### Edge Cases
6. rustc exit codes:
   - Direct rustc command failures
   - Verify: exit codes match local behavior

7. gcc exit codes:
   - gcc compilation errors
   - Verify: standard gcc exit codes

8. make exit codes:
   - make failure
   - Verify: 2 (make error) vs other codes

## Verification Method
For each test:
1. Run command locally, record exit code
2. Run command via rch, record exit code
3. Assert: codes are identical

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exit 0 means success
- Exit 1 means build/compilation error
- Exit 101 means cargo test failures
- Signal exits (128+N) correctly propagated
- No exit code transformation or loss

## MANDATORY Logging

All tests MUST use TestLogger with structured JSON output:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"setup","msg":"Creating valid build project"}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"local_baseline","msg":"Local execution","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"remote_execution","msg":"Remote execution","data":{"worker":"css","exit_code":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code comparison","data":{"local":0,"remote":0,"match":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_failure","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code comparison","data":{"local":101,"remote":101,"match":true,"code_type":"test_failure"}}
```

### Required Logging Points
1. Test setup phase with fixture preparation
2. Local baseline execution with exit code
3. Remote execution with worker and exit code
4. Verification comparison (local vs remote)
5. Signal handling events when testing signal exits
6. Clear PASS/FAIL verdict with exit code details","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:12.965225919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T08:29:53.688980658Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T08:29:53.688934181Z","close_reason":"Implemented exit_code_tests.rs with 10 test cases: cargo build/test success (0), build error (101), test failures (101), rustc success/error (0/1), gcc success/error (0/2 via make), make error (2), signal exits (SIGTERM 143, SIGKILL 137), and arbitrary exit codes. Created fixtures: broken_c, broken_make, broken_rustc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yj8","depends_on_id":"bd-10g8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:28.658618279Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yj8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ga8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:26.948898595Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1yt6","title":"Implement remote build cancellation + deterministic cleanup semantics","description":"Implement explicit cancellation handling for remote builds so interrupted/aborted jobs are terminated safely and system state is restored deterministically. This prevents slot leaks, orphaned processes, stale temp state, and confusing operator signals after user/agent cancellations or timeout-driven aborts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cancellation signal propagates deterministically across hook/daemon/worker execution flow with stable reason codes.
- Remote execution cancellation uses bounded escalation (graceful stop, then enforced termination) within policy guardrails.
- Post-cancel cleanup reclaims worker slot/accounting state, temporary workdirs, and cancellation debt markers without broad/destructive actions.
- Cancellation outcomes are reflected in status/doctor surfaces with actionable remediation guidance.
- Unit tests cover cancellation state machine branches (pre-start, in-flight, post-completion race, repeated cancel).
- Integration tests cover cancellation under network jitter, worker unreachability, and partial transfer/exec states.
- E2E scripts validate repeated cancellation storms, mixed cancellation+disk-pressure+triage scenarios, and deterministic fallback behavior.
- Structured logs include cancel origin, operation id, decision path, escalation stage, cleanup result, and final worker health state.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:46.868356050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51.391195585Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","e2e","operations","rch-rel-2026q1","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.695690869Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.075612128Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.278610534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.492433017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.896328665Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.098293065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.300398679Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization revision: cancellation is now first-class reliability behavior with explicit cleanup and observability guarantees, reducing user-facing flakiness from aborted remote builds.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1yt6","title":"Implement remote build cancellation + deterministic cleanup semantics","description":"Implement explicit cancellation handling for remote builds so interrupted/aborted jobs are terminated safely and system state is restored deterministically. This prevents slot leaks, orphaned processes, stale temp state, and confusing operator signals after user/agent cancellations or timeout-driven aborts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cancellation signal propagates deterministically across hook/daemon/worker execution flow with stable reason codes.
- Remote execution cancellation uses bounded escalation (graceful stop, then enforced termination) within policy guardrails.
- Post-cancel cleanup reclaims worker slot/accounting state, temporary workdirs, and cancellation debt markers without broad/destructive actions.
- Cancellation outcomes are reflected in status/doctor surfaces with actionable remediation guidance.
- Unit tests cover cancellation state machine branches (pre-start, in-flight, post-completion race, repeated cancel).
- Integration tests cover cancellation under network jitter, worker unreachability, and partial transfer/exec states.
- E2E scripts validate repeated cancellation storms, mixed cancellation+disk-pressure+triage scenarios, and deterministic fallback behavior.
- Structured logs include cancel origin, operation id, decision path, escalation stage, cleanup result, and final worker health state.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:46.868356050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T06:12:06.834590717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","e2e","operations","rch-rel-2026q1","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.695690869Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.075612128Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.278610534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.492433017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:47.896328665Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.098293065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.300398679Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-1yt6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization revision: cancellation is now first-class reliability behavior with explicit cleanup and observability guarantees, reducing user-facing flakiness from aborted remote builds.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z6p","title":"Phase 3: Daemon UI - Rich Output for rchd Server","description":"Add rich terminal output to the rchd daemon for operators monitoring the server. This phase covers: startup banner with version and configuration summary, job lifecycle logging with structured formatting, worker fleet status updates, performance metrics dashboard, and graceful shutdown messaging. The daemon serves two audiences: (1) operators watching logs in a terminal, and (2) log aggregation systems parsing structured logs. Rich output targets the first audience via stderr while structured logs continue to stdout/files.

Key considerations:
- Daemon may run in foreground (interactive) or background (systemd) - detect and adjust
- Log output must remain parseable for log aggregation (JSON structured logging untouched)
- Rich output should enhance human readability without breaking existing tooling
- Support RCHD_RICH_OUTPUT=0 to completely disable rich formatting
- Performance-critical: daemon handles many jobs/sec, rich output must not bottleneck","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:03.759129972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:47:47.125756021Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:47:47.125679689Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z6p","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
